@@ -87,7 +87,7 @@ {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5zm","title":"Implement report_failure() with structured output","description":"# Task: Implement report_failure() with structured output ## Context Part of EPIC: Per-Phase Error Reporting (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fkf) ## What to Do Create a comprehensive failure report function: ### Human Output (terminal) ``` ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ ║ ✖ INSTALLATION FAILED ║ ║ ║ ║ Phase 6/10: Language Runtimes ║ ║ Failed at: Installing Rust via rustup ║ ║ ║ ║ Error: curl: (7) Failed to connect to sh.rustup.rs ║ ║ ║ ║ Suggested Fix: ║ ║ 1. Check internet: curl -I https://google.com ║ ║ 2. Retry: curl ... | bash -s -- --resume ║ ║ ║ ║ Full log: /var/log/acfs/install.log ║ ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ ``` ### JSON Output (log file) Append structured JSON to install.log for tooling. ### With gum Use gum style for prettier box drawing. ## Acceptance Criteria - Shows phase number and name - Shows specific step that failed - Shows error excerpt (truncated if long) - Shows suggested fix from ERROR_PATTERNS - Shows resume command - Works with and without gum ## Files to Modify - install.sh: Add report_failure() function","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:44:00.671428-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:47:14.92113-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T14:47:14.92113-05:00","close_reason":"Created scripts/lib/report.sh with report_failure(), report_success(), report_warning(), report_skipped_summary(). Supports gum styling, JSON logging, and suggested fixes from ERROR_PATTERNS.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5zm","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-vwv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:47:30.834519-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5zm","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-qqo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:47:31.011809-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5zt","title":"Design state.json v2 schema","description":"# Task: Design state.json v2 schema ## Context Part of EPIC: Phase-Granular Progress Persistence ## Critical Design Decision: Track Phase IDs, Not Numbers **Problem with tracking phase numbers:** If we store `completed_phases: [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]` and later ACFS reorders phases or adds a new phase 3, the resume logic would skip the wrong phases. **Solution:** Track phase IDs (stable identifiers): ```json { "schema_version": 2, "completed_phases": ["user_setup", "apt_packages", "shell_setup", "cli_tools", "languages"], "current_phase": "agents", ... } ``` ## Full Schema ```json { "schema_version": 2, "version": "0.1.0", "mode": "vibe", "started_at": "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z", "last_updated": "2025-01-15T10:42:00Z", "completed_phases": ["user_setup", "apt_packages", "shell_setup"], "current_phase": "cli_tools", "current_step": "Installing ripgrep", "failed_phase": null, "failed_step": null, "skipped_tools": ["ntm", "bv"], "skipped_phases": ["postgres", "vault"], "phase_durations": { "user_setup": 12, "apt_packages": 45 } } ``` ## Phase IDs (stable, never change meaning) - user_setup - apt_packages - shell_setup - cli_tools - languages - tools - database - cloud - agents - stack - finalize ## Backwards Compatibility - If schema_version missing or 1: treat as legacy, offer fresh start - Phase IDs are strings, won't conflict with old number-based format ## Acceptance Criteria - Schema uses phase IDs not numbers - Documented in code comments - Backwards compatible with v1 detection - All fields have sensible defaults","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:42:39.486596-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:39:43.137058-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T14:39:43.137058-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented in scripts/lib/state.sh. Schema v2 uses phase IDs, is documented, and has backwards compatibility."} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-6fx","title":"cloud_db.sh: verify respects SKIP flags + optional installs","description":"scripts/lib/cloud_db.sh claims to respect SKIP_POSTGRES/SKIP_VAULT/SKIP_CLOUD in install_all_cloud_db, but verify_cloud_db still fails if those tools are intentionally skipped.","acceptance_criteria":"- With SKIP_POSTGRES=true, verify_cloud_db does not mark psql missing as failure. - With SKIP_VAULT=true, verify_cloud_db does not mark vault missing as failure. - With SKIP_CLOUD=true, verify_cloud_db does not mark cloud CLIs missing as failure.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-20T14:45:03.242319-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T14:52:41.60688-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T14:52:41.60688-05:00","close_reason":"Updated verify_cloud_db to honor SKIP_POSTGRES/SKIP_VAULT/SKIP_CLOUD so skips don't count as failures."} -{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-6pto","title":"Task: Add credit card and email verification warnings to rent-vps","description":"","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:46:08.892768-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T23:46:08.892768-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-6pto","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-qxj8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:52:38.507419-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} +{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-6pto","title":"Task: Add credit card and email verification warnings to rent-vps","description":"","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:46:08.892768-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T13:13:56.059571-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-6pto","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-qxj8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:52:38.507419-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-6r9o","title":"Enhance terminal copy/paste instructions with dedicated section","description":"# Task: Enhance Terminal Copy/Paste Instructions ## Parent Epic [EPIC] Terminal Onboarding Foundation (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ux1f) ## Current State Copy/paste instructions currently exist in: - generate-ssh-key/page.tsx Step 3 (Mac and Windows paste shortcuts) - ssh-connect/page.tsx Step 3 (same) However, users encounter copy/paste FIRST in install-terminal when downloading, so a dedicated section there would help. ## Enhancement Add a dedicated GuideSection in install-terminal/page.tsx SimplerGuide with comprehensive copy/paste instructions: ```tsx

Terminal copy/paste works a bit differently than other apps:

Mac (Ghostty/WezTerm)

  • ⌘ + C to copy
  • ⌘ + V to paste
  • Works just like other Mac apps!

Windows (Windows Terminal)

  • Right-click to paste (easiest!)
  • Or use Ctrl + Shift + C/V
  • Note: Ctrl + C STOPS commands, it does not copy!
 ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Consolidates copy/paste instructions in install-terminal (first encounter) - [ ] Mac instructions are clear and simple - [ ] Windows instructions warn about Ctrl+C danger - [ ] Uses kbd styling for key combinations - [ ] OS-conditional display (show only relevant OS) ## Note This enhances existing scattered mentions with a dedicated, comprehensive section.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:39:41.923436-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:21:11.767553-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:21:11.767553-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented: TerminalBasicsSection in install-terminal/page.tsx has dedicated copy/paste section with Mac (cmd+C/V) and Windows (right-click, Ctrl+Shift+V) instructions, including Ctrl+C warning for Windows"} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-6stg","title":"Task: Add SSH disconnect warning with reconnection instructions","description":"","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:47:15.391045-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T23:47:15.391045-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-6stg","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r1z5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:52:49.078227-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-6w9u","title":"Create dedicated /glossary page","description":"# Task: Create Dedicated Glossary Page ## Parent Epic [EPIC] Jargon System & Documentation Polish (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-7i45) ## Description Create a comprehensive, searchable glossary page where users can look up technical terms. This provides a deeper reference than tooltips and helps users who want to understand the concepts more fully. ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/glossary/page.tsx \`\`\`tsx // Key features: // 1. Alphabetical listing of all terms // 2. Search/filter functionality // 3. Categorization (Shell, Networking, Tools, etc.) // 4. Detailed explanations (more than tooltip) // 5. Links to relevant wizard steps or lessons interface GlossaryEntry { term: string; short: string; // Tooltip version long: string; // Full explanation category: 'shell' | 'networking' | 'tools' | 'concepts'; relatedTerms?: string[]; learnMore?: string; // Link to lesson } \`\`\` Page should include: - Search box at top - Category filters - Clean, scannable layout - Mobile-friendly (single column) - Links from jargon tooltips to glossary entries ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All terms from Jargon component included - [ ] Search filters results in real-time - [ ] Categories help organize terms - [ ] Long explanations are thorough but accessible - [ ] Links to related wizard steps where applicable - [ ] Mobile-responsive design ## UI/UX Notes - This is a reference page, not a learning page - Users might land here from Google searches - Consider SEO implications (good meta descriptions) - Use same visual style as rest of site","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:45:10.252228-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:43:47.598957-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:43:47.598957-05:00","close_reason":"Completed (new /glossary page with live search + category filters; added Jargon tooltip links to glossary anchors)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-6w9u","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-367d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:57:43.702732-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} @@ -285,7 +285,7 @@ {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-qxj8","title":"Feature: Pre-Installation Clarity - Landing Page & Early Wizard","description":"","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:42:45.335788-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T13:11:48.986295-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T13:11:48.986295-05:00","close_reason":"Added Why VPS? {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r2np","title":"[EPIC] Post-Install Transition Experience","description":"# Epic: Post-Install Transition Experience ## Problem Statement After installation, users must reconnect as ubuntu (instead of root). This transition has several pitfalls: 1. **New SSH command format**: The reconnect command uses `-i ~/.ssh/acfs_ed25519` which is NEW. Users don't understand they're now using key auth instead of password. 2. **Key auth failure**: If key auth doesn't work, they're stuck. No clear fallback path. 3. **Powerlevel10k wizard surprise**: On first zsh login, p10k may launch a configuration wizard. This is unexpected and confusing. 4. **Prompt change disorientation**: The prompt looks completely different now (colorful p10k vs plain bash). Users might think something is wrong. ## Background & Reasoning The root → ubuntu transition is a security best practice, but it creates a jarring experience. Users went from password auth as root to key auth as ubuntu. The shell also changes from bash to zsh with p10k theming. Each of these changes is an improvement, but together they make the VPS feel like a completely different machine. Users need preparation for these changes. ## User Story As a user reconnecting after installation, I want to understand the changes to my VPS, So that I'm not confused by the new authentication method, shell, and prompt. ## Success Criteria 1. User understands the new SSH command uses their key 2. User knows they won't need a password anymore 3. User knows to press 'q' if p10k wizard appears 4. User understands the colorful prompt is normal and good 5. User has a fallback if key auth fails ## Scope - Enhance reconnect-ubuntu page with key vs password explanation - Add p10k wizard handling instructions - Add "Your prompt looks different now" explanation - Add key auth troubleshooting - Add fallback instructions if key doesn't work ## Dependencies - Epic F: Installation (user must have completed installation) ## UI/UX Requirements - Maintain current polish for desktop and mobile - Key auth explanation should be clear but not overwhelming - P10k handling should be a GuideTip - Prompt change should include before/after visual ## Technical Approach 1. Add explanation of key auth vs password above SSH command 2. Add GuideTip about p10k wizard (press q to skip) 3. Add OutputPreview showing new prompt style 4. Add troubleshooting section for key auth failures 5. Consider adding visual comparison: old prompt vs new ## Estimated Effort Small-Medium (1 wizard page enhancement) ## Priority Justification P1 (High) - Reconnection failure after successful install is very frustrating.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:36:12.00915-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:55:04.567741-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:55:04.567741-05:00","close_reason":"All 5 success criteria verified: (1) key auth explanation in reconnect-ubuntu:131-141, (2) 'No password needed' AlertCard:143-146, (3) p10k wizard handling:170-189, (4) colorful prompt explained:165,203,235, (5) key auth fallback/troubleshooting in verify-key-connection:120-137","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r2np","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-vydu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:38:04.85656-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r3gd","title":"Align agent aliases across zshrc and docs","description":"Found mismatch: zsh aliases for cod/gmi didn't match AGENTS/README/onboarding. Update acfs/zsh/acfs.zshrc, onboarding lesson 04, and agent command examples to use codex --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox and gemini --yolo; align README snippet.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:15:58.950788-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T19:30:11.495408-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T19:30:11.495408-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 45ea80d - namespaced SCRIPT_DIR and aligned agent aliases"} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r50f","title":"State library: fail clearly on jq update errors","description":"scripts/lib/state.sh has several jq command substitutions that don’t check exit status (e.g., state_step_update/state_phase_complete/state_phase_fail/state_save/state_migrate_v1_to_v2/state_upgrade_set_error). In non-strict callers this can silently propagate bad state updates. Add explicit error handling so failures return nonzero without writing invalid/empty state.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-22T22:54:49.303573-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T22:59:19.847092-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T22:59:19.847092-05:00","close_reason":"Completed: added explicit jq failure handling in state.sh write/update helpers and initialized selection arrays early in install.sh; shellcheck + bash -n passed."} @@ -307,7 +307,7 @@ {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-snxd","title":"TASK: Integrate dashboard commands into acfs CLI","description":"# TASK: Integrate dashboard commands into acfs CLI ## Context Wire up dashboard subcommands into acfs CLI. ## Implementation ### Update acfs() function ```bash acfs() { case "$1" in dashboard|dash) shift case "$1" in generate) acfs_dashboard_generate "${@:2}" ;; serve) acfs_dashboard_serve "${@:2}" ;; *) echo "Usage: acfs dashboard [options]" ;; esac ;; # ... esac } ``` ### Update help text ``` acfs dashboard generate Generate static HTML dashboard acfs dashboard serve Serve dashboard via HTTP ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] acfs dashboard generate works - [ ] acfs dashboard serve works - [ ] acfs dash (alias) works - [ ] Help text updated - [ ] Error handling for invalid subcommands","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T15:01:00.112921-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T15:40:00.465006-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T15:40:00.465006-05:00","close_reason":"Added dash alias and updated help text for dashboard command","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-snxd","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-x01y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T15:01:22.833645-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ss3","title":"Add --json output mode to security.sh --verify","description":"# Task: Add --json output mode to security.sh --verify ## Context Part of EPIC: Automated Upstream Checksum Monitoring (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-4ou) ## What to Do Enhance security.sh --verify to output JSON for automation: ### Command ```bash ./scripts/lib/security.sh --verify --json ``` ### Output Format ```json { "timestamp": "2025-01-15T06:00:00Z", "total": 14, "matches": [ {"name": "bun", "checksum": "abc123..."} ], "mismatches": [ { "name": "ntm", "expected": "abc123...", "actual": "def456...", "url": "https://..." } ], "errors": [ {"name": "broken", "error": "Fetch failed"} ] } ``` ### Implementation Create verify_all_json() function that builds JSON output. ## Acceptance Criteria - --json flag produces valid JSON - All checksums verified and categorized - Mismatches include old and new values - Errors captured (network failures, etc.) ## Files to Modify - scripts/lib/security.sh","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:45:51.791796-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:48:46.932748-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T14:48:46.932748-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented verify_all_installers_json() function with --json flag for automated verification. Outputs matches, mismatches, errors, and skipped arrays with timestamps."} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-t0f","title":"[codex.7] Update onboard lesson 04 with account type clarification","description":"## Goal Update acfs/onboard/lessons/04_agents_login.md to clarify the ChatGPT account requirement. ## Current State The lesson already correctly describes running "codex auth" but doesn't explain: - That a ChatGPT Pro/Plus subscription is needed (not API account) - The difference between API accounts and ChatGPT accounts - That users may need to enable device access in ChatGPT settings ## Updates Needed 1. Add a note about ChatGPT Pro/Plus account requirement 2. Mention the difference from API accounts 3. Add troubleshooting if auth fails (check ChatGPT security settings) 4. Keep the existing correct auth command instructions ## Context for Users Many ACFS users may be developers familiar with OpenAI's API who: - Have API accounts with OPENAI_API_KEY - May NOT have ChatGPT Pro subscriptions - Need to understand these are separate things ## Acceptance Criteria 1. Clear mention of ChatGPT Pro/Plus account requirement 2. Troubleshooting guidance for common issues 3. No confusion with API accounts","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:26:23.414622-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T16:44:37.704066-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T16:44:37.704066-05:00","close_reason":"Updated onboard lesson 04: clarified ChatGPT Pro/Plus requirement, added distinction from API accounts, fixed command to codex login","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-t0f","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ua5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:26:36.773325-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} -{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-t0gk","title":"Task: Add 'Why VPS?' section to landing page","description":"","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:46:03.504584-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T23:46:03.504584-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-t0gk","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-qxj8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:52:33.220551-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} +{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-t0gk","title":"Task: Add 'Why VPS?' section to landing page","description":"","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:46:03.504584-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T13:13:16.916229-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T13:13:16.916229-05:00","close_reason":"Completed by BrownStone as part of qxj8 - Why VPS section added in commit for qxj8","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-t0gk","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-qxj8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:52:33.220551-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-t3c","title":"Task: Port workflow lessons (7-8) to webapp","description":"# Port Workflow Lessons (7-8) to Webapp ## Parent Feature Lesson Content Pages (cxk) ## Scope Create lesson pages for the capstone workflow lessons: - Lesson 7: The Flywheel Loop (`07_flywheel_loop.md`) - Lesson 8: Keeping Updated (`08_keeping_updated.md`) ## Why Grouped These are the culmination lessons: - Lesson 7 ties everything together - Lesson 8 ensures long-term success - Both are shorter and reference earlier content ## Lesson-Specific Enhancements ### Lesson 7: The Flywheel Loop This is the most important conceptual lesson - it explains how all 8 tools work together. Enhancements: - Interactive flywheel diagram (could reuse/adapt existing flywheel-visualization.tsx) - Tool quick-reference cards for each of the 8 tools - Complete workflow example with step indicators - Links to each tool's detailed documentation Key Visual: ``` Plan (Beads) --> Coordinate (Agent Mail) --> Execute (NTM + Agents) ^ | | v +---- Remember (CASS Memory) <---- Scan (UBS) + ``` The 8 Tools Covered: 1. NTM - Orchestration 2. MCP Agent Mail - Coordination 3. UBS - Quality scanning 4. CASS - Session search 5. CASS Memory (CM) - Procedural memory 6. Beads Viewer (BV) - Task management 7. CAAM - Account switching 8. SLB - Safety guardrails ### Lesson 8: Keeping Updated Final lesson on maintenance and updates. Enhancements: - Update command reference table - System health checklist component - Links to release notes / changelog - "What's new" section (could be dynamic) Key Commands: ```bash acfs-update # Update all tools acfs doctor # Health check onboard # Re-run tutorial ``` ## Connection to /workflow Page The existing `/workflow` page is "Part Two: The Agentic Workflow" which goes deeper than Lesson 7. Consider: - Lesson 7 = Introduction to the workflow (Learning Hub) - /workflow = Deep dive with scenarios, prompts, architecture Add clear handoff: "Ready for advanced techniques? [Continue to The Agentic Workflow →](/workflow)" ## Celebratory Completion When user completes Lesson 8: - Show completion confetti (reuse from Step 12) - Display achievement summary - Clear call-to-action: "You've completed the basics! Start building!" - Links to: - `/workflow` for advanced techniques - GitHub repo for reference - Community resources (if any) ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Both workflow lessons accessible - [ ] Flywheel diagram renders (interactive preferred) - [ ] All 8 tools listed with command references - [ ] Complete workflow example shows full cycle - [ ] Lesson 8 completion triggers celebration - [ ] Clear navigation to /workflow ## Dependencies Same as other lesson tasks.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:21:46.413905-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T18:26:23.584959-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T18:26:23.584959-05:00","close_reason":"All 9 lessons already load and render from source markdown files via lesson-content.tsx. No separate porting needed.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-t3c","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ori","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:24:09.959352-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-t3c","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-25i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:24:10.153969-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-t3c","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-j9a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:24:10.358651-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-t66c","title":"FEATURE: Wizard Password-First Flow Updates","description":"## Parent Epic agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-umqa (Beginner UX Overhaul v1.0) ## Summary Update all wizard pages to reflect the new password-first SSH flow. This involves: - Simplifying the "Create VPS" page (no SSH key paste) - Adding explicit "First Connection with Password" guidance - Adding "Verify Key-Based Connection" step - Updating all SSH commands and expectations ## Background & Motivation The current wizard flow assumes users will paste their SSH key into the VPS provider's UI. The audit revealed this is a critical failure point - users can't find where to paste the key. With the installer now handling SSH key setup (see FEATURE: Installer SSH Key Integration), we need to update the wizard to reflect this simpler flow: 1. Create VPS → Just pick Ubuntu, note your password 2. First connection → SSH with password (not key) 3. Run installer → Installer prompts for key 4. Verify → Disconnect and reconnect with key ## Pages Requiring Updates ### 1. /wizard/generate-ssh-key (Step 3) **Current**: "Copy your public key for the next step" **New**: "Save your public key to notes - you'll paste it during installation" Changes: - Add emphasis on SAVING the key, not just copying - Clarify that the key is used LATER, not immediately - Add note: "Don't worry about pasting it into provider's website" ### 2. /wizard/create-vps (Step 5) **Current**: Has checklist including "Pasted my SSH public key" **New**: Simplified checklist focusing on password Changes: - Remove SSH key paste instruction - Add: "Set a strong root password (write it down!)" - Add: "Note your server's IP address" - Simplify to just: Ubuntu + password + IP Provider-specific guidance: - "Skip any SSH key sections - we'll set that up later" - "If asked about authentication, choose 'Password'" ### 3. /wizard/ssh-connect (Step 6) **Current**: Uses key-based SSH command **New**: Uses password-based SSH command Changes: - Primary command: `ssh root@` (no -i flag) - Add expectation: "It will ask for your password" - Update troubleshooting for password-based connection - Add note: "This is the last time you'll need the password" ### 4. NEW: Installer Step (absorbs old Step 9) Move installer step earlier in flow (right after first connection). Changes: - Add note: "The installer will ask for your SSH key" - Add expectation: "Have your public key ready to paste" - Link back to where they saved their key ### 5. NEW: /wizard/verify-key-connection (New Step) After installer runs, verify key-based connection works. Content: - "Disconnect from your VPS: type `exit`" - "Now reconnect WITH your key:" - Command: `ssh -i ~/.ssh/acfs_ed25519 ubuntu@` - "If this works without asking for password, you're all set!" - Troubleshooting for common key issues ### 6. /wizard/launch-onboarding (Final Step) Add reconnection instructions prominently. Changes: - Add "How to Get Back In" section at top - Include both commands (key-based for ubuntu) - Add SSH config setup suggestion ## Step Number Renumbering Current flow (12 steps): 1. OS Selection 2. Install Terminal 3. Generate SSH Key 4. Rent VPS 5. Create VPS ← simplified 6. SSH Connect ← password-based now 7. Accounts 8. Preflight Check 9. Run Installer ← moves earlier 10. Status Check 11. Reconnect Ubuntu 12. Launch Onboarding New flow (proposed): 1. OS Selection 2. Install Terminal 3. Generate SSH Key 4. Rent VPS 5. Create VPS (password-based) 6. First Connection (password) 7. Run Installer (prompts for key) 8. Verify Key Connection (new) 9. Accounts (simplified) 10. Launch Onboarding (enhanced) Net reduction: 12 → 10 steps (cleaner!) ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] /wizard/generate-ssh-key emphasizes saving key for later - [ ] /wizard/create-vps removes SSH key paste requirement - [ ] /wizard/ssh-connect uses password-based command - [ ] New verify-key-connection page exists - [ ] All step numbers updated - [ ] Progress indicator reflects new step count - [ ] All SimplerGuide sections updated for new flow - [ ] Troubleshooting sections updated ## Dependencies - FEATURE: Installer SSH Key Integration (must be done first) - The wizard updates assume the installer prompts for key ## Design Considerations 1. **Preserve Beginner Guidance**: Keep SimplerGuide sections but update content 2. **Mobile Friendly**: Ensure all new pages work on mobile 3. **No Breaking Changes to URLs**: Use redirects if step slugs change 4. **Analytics Continuity**: Update step names in analytics tracking ## Testing Plan 1. Walk through entire wizard as complete beginner 2. Test on new VPS with password-only setup 3. Verify key verification step works 4. Test with both Contabo and OVH 5. Check mobile responsiveness","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:15:55.355583-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T19:40:08.582429-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T19:40:08.582429-05:00","close_reason":"Wizard pages updated for password-first flow: generate-ssh-key, create-vps, ssh-connect all updated; verify-key-connection page created","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-t66c","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fslp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:16:33.957554-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-t66c","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-umqa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:16:34.122624-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-t9i","title":"EPIC: Enhanced Doctor with Functional Tests","description":"# Epic: Enhanced Doctor with Functional Tests ## Overview Add --deep flag to acfs doctor that performs functional smoke tests, not just binary existence checks. ## Problem Statement acfs doctor only checks if binaries exist, not if they work. It says 'All critical checks passed' but cc immediately fails with 'not authenticated'. Binary existence ≠ functional. ## Business Impact - Auth issues are the most common post-install failure - Users copy aliases but skip authentication - Deep checks catch drift (tokens expire, configs corrupted) - Reduces 'it says it passed but nothing works' support tickets ## Technical Approach 1. Add --deep flag (default doctor stays fast) 2. Agent auth checks: Claude config exists + test call, Codex OPENAI_API_KEY, Gemini credentials 3. Database checks: PostgreSQL connection and role verification 4. Cloud CLI checks: Vault configured, Wrangler/Supabase/Vercel auth status 5. Token expiration warnings where applicable 6. Cache results to avoid rate limiting ## Success Criteria - --deep catches all auth configuration issues - Each check has actionable fix suggestion - 4 status levels: pass, warn, fail, info (skip) - Maintains backwards compatibility (default doctor unchanged) ## Files to Modify - scripts/lib/doctor.sh: Add --deep flag and functional tests ## Reference See: docs/ROADMAP_INSTALLER_RELIABILITY.md Section 5","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:41:13.234724-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:16:10.006918-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T15:16:10.006918-05:00","close_reason":"EPIC complete - all children implemented: --deep flag (01s), agent auth checks (325), DB/cloud CLI checks (azw), run_deep_checks output (aqs), timeout/caching (lz1)","comments":[{"id":5,"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-t9i","author":"jemanuel","text":"FYI: created `agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt` (Manifest-Driven Installer Integration). This work will refactor `install.sh` to source generated installers + manifest_index and will touch selection semantics (`--only/--skip/--print-plan`) and curl|bash bootstrap (single archive). Please keep this epic’s work compatible with your scope: - orchestration/state/error-reporting/preflight remains install.sh + scripts/lib owned - generated scripts stay source-safe (no top-level side effects)","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:50:07Z"}]}
